county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Van Wert. Created 1820, organized 1837. Located in the Black Swamp region — very late settlement. Named for Isaac Van Wert, one of the captors of Major John Andre. Van Wert County Historical Society. Flat topography meant extensive drainage records document land improvements. Strong German heritage — Willshire, Ohio City, and Middle Point church records. Marsh area lands were some of the last to be settled. Check Darke County for early records.

source_profiles.repository: Van Wert County Recorder, Van Wert
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Van Wert County Probate Court, Van Wert
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1820. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Van Wert County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Van Wert County Probate Court, Van Wert
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1820. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Van Wert County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Van Wert County Clerk of Courts, Van Wert
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1820. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

county_metadata.parent_county: Athens, Gallia, Hocking, Jackson, Meigs, Ross
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1850 from Athens, Gallia, Hocking, Jackson, Meigs, Ross.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in McArthur. Created 1850 from Athens, Gallia, Hocking, Jackson, Meigs, and Ross — check all six parent counties for earlier records. Named for Samuel Finley Vinton, Ohio congressman. Ohio's least populous county. Vinton County Historical and Genealogical Society. Lake Hope and Zaleski State Forests. Iron furnace records (Hope Furnace, Richland Furnace). Very rural Appalachian county — church records (Methodist, Baptist) essential. Congress Lands.

source_profiles.repository: Vinton County Recorder, McArthur
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1850. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Vinton County Probate Court, McArthur
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1850. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Vinton County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Vinton County Probate Court, McArthur
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1850. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Hanging Rock Iron Region Records — Vinton County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Charcoal iron furnace records including worker rolls, company store ledgers, and furnace operation logs. The Hanging Rock Iron Region in southern Ohio employed thousands of workers including Welsh, English, and African American laborers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered — some at Ohio History Connection, some at local museums (e.g., Buckeye Furnace State Memorial)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Lebanon. One of Ohio's earliest counties (1803). Located in the Symmes Purchase and Virginia Military District. Lebanon is one of Ohio's most well-preserved historic towns. Warren County Historical Society has excellent collections including Shaker records — Union Village (1805-1912) was the largest Shaker community in the West. Golden Lamb Inn (1803) is Ohio's oldest continuously operating inn — guest registers. Otterbein Cemetery records. Check Hamilton County for pre-1803 records.

source_profiles.repository: Warren County Recorder, Lebanon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1803. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Warren County Probate Court, Lebanon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1803. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Warren County Probate Court, Lebanon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1803. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Warren County Clerk of Courts, Lebanon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1803. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Warren County Auditor, Lebanon / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1803. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Military District Land Warrants — Warren County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Symmes Purchase Land Records — Warren County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Cincinnati History Library and Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: John Cleves Symmes purchased approximately 1 million acres between the Great Miami and Little Miami Rivers. Records document original patent holders and early settlers in SW Ohio.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records at Ohio History Connection and Cincinnati History Library and Archives

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Warren County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / NARA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

source_profiles.source_name: Society of Friends (Quaker) Meeting Records — Warren County
source_profiles.repository: Wilmington College Quaker Heritage Center / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Monthly meeting records include births, marriages, deaths, removals (transfers), and disownments. Quakers kept meticulous records. Ohio Yearly Meeting and Indiana Yearly Meeting records at Wilmington College.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptionally well-preserved; many microfilmed and indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Marietta. Ohio's first county (1788). Marietta was the first permanent American settlement in the Northwest Territory (1788). Campus Martius Museum. Ohio Company of Associates land records are essential — available at Marietta College archives and Ohio History Connection. Washington County Historical Society. Mound Cemetery (Revolutionary War officers buried here). Extensive early court records from 1788. Marietta College archives (1835). Oil springs in Macksburg area.

source_profiles.repository: Washington County Recorder, Marietta
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1788. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1788. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Ohio Company of Associates Land Records — Washington County
source_profiles.repository: Marietta College Archives / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Ohio Company of Associates (organized 1786 in Massachusetts) purchased 1.5 million acres in SE Ohio. Records document original purchasers, many from New England. Marietta was the first permanent settlement.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Marietta College Special Collections and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / NARA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Wooster. One of the original Northwest Territory counties (1796). College of Wooster (1866) archives. Wayne County Historical Society. OARDC (Ohio Agricultural Research and Development Center) in Wooster — agricultural records. Strong Amish and Mennonite communities in southern Wayne County — church records essential. Congress Lands and Western Reserve boundary area. Wayne County was massive originally — spawned many other counties. Extensive early records.

source_profiles.repository: Wayne County Recorder, Wooster
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1796. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.

source_profiles.source_name: Amish and Mennonite Church Records — Wayne County
source_profiles.repository: Heritage Historical Library, Aylmer ON / Mennonite Historical Library, Goshen IN / Local church records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Amish church district records, family group sheets, Budget newspaper correspondence, and Mennonite church records. Ohio has the largest Amish population in the world. Records may be in German/Pennsylvanian Dutch. Die Botschaft and The Budget newspapers document Amish community events.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Amish records remain in private family collections; Heritage Historical Library has compiled indexes

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Bryan. Created 1820, organized 1824. Located in the Black Swamp region — late settlement. Williams County Historical Society. Named for David Williams, a captor of Major John Andre. Lake area — Lake La Su An, Nettle Lake. Strong German heritage — community church records important. Bryan was an important railroad junction — railroad employment records. Northwest Ohio flatlands — drainage improvement records. Check Darke County for early records.

source_profiles.repository: Williams County Recorder, Bryan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Bowling Green. Created 1820, organized 1823. Located in the Black Swamp region — settlement followed Black Swamp drainage. Bowling Green State University (1910) archives. Wood County Historical Center and Museum. Fort Meigs (Perrysburg) is the largest walled fortification in the US — War of 1812 records. Perrysburg is one of the oldest towns in NW Ohio. Strong German heritage. Oil and gas boom (1880s-1890s) records. Maumee River valley.

source_profiles.repository: Wood County Recorder, Bowling Green
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Wood County Clerk of Courts, Bowling Green
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1820. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.

source_profiles.repository: Wood County Clerk of Courts, Bowling Green / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection, Columbus
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Ohio History Connection

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Upper Sandusky. Created 1845 from Marion, Hardin, and Crawford. Named for the Wyandot (Huron) people — Upper Sandusky was the Wyandot capital. Wyandot removal (1843) records at NARA — the last Native American removal from Ohio. Wyandot County Historical Society. Wyandot Mission Church records. Cranberry Bog (unique geological feature). Check parent counties for pre-1845 records. Strong German immigrant community arrived after Wyandot removal.

source_profiles.repository: Wyandot County Recorder, Upper Sandusky
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1845. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation
